name: Extubation
description: Removal of an airway tube (endotracheal or tracheostomy), including unplanned removal; tube changes are excluded.

en.name: Extubation
en.shortDescription: Removal of an airway tube (endotracheal or tracheostomy), including unplanned removal; tube changes are excluded.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Extubation is the removal of an artificial airway tube — an endotracheal tube (passed through the mouth or nose into the trachea) or a tracheostomy tube (inserted through a surgical opening in the neck) — that was in place to support breathing. The source data is an event record: a procedure (or a recorded clinical finding, in the case of an unplanned removal) marking the moment the tube was taken out, rather than a numeric measurement. It is recorded in intensive care, recovery, and ward settings, typically as part of weaning a patient off mechanical ventilation. There is no unit — each record represents one extubation event for a patient at a timestamp.

The clinically important distinction in this set is between **removing** a tube (extubation — the patient is left without an artificial airway) and **changing** a tube (the old tube is taken out and a new one inserted immediately, so the patient is never without an airway). A tube change is *not* an extubation and is excluded from this set (see Excluded Concepts).

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED procedure *Extubation of trachea* with all its descendants, plus the separate clinical finding for unplanned extubation. Excluding the tube-change branch (below), this resolves to five concepts, grouped here by what they describe.

### Generic extubation (default)

Use this when the source records an extubation without specifying which device was removed or how.

- *4148972 / 309812005 — Extubation of trachea (SNOMED)*: the general concept for removing an airway tube from the trachea. Synonym: "Tracheal extubation".

### By airway device removed

Use these when the source explicitly identifies the device.

- *4150627 / 271280005 — Removal of endotracheal tube (SNOMED)*: removal of an endotracheal tube specifically. Synonym: "Endotracheal extubation".
- *4195943 / 44482009 — Ventilating tube removal by another physician (SNOMED)*: a tube removal carried out by a physician other than the one primarily responsible; a child of endotracheal tube removal in SNOMED. Map here only when the source explicitly records this circumstance.
- *4012607 / 113153002 — Removal of tracheostomy tube (SNOMED)*: removal of a tracheostomy tube (decannulation).

### Unplanned extubation

- *4231838 / 405639008 — Inadvertent tracheal extubation (SNOMED)*: an accidental or unintended removal of the airway tube (e.g. self-extubation by the patient, or dislodgement during care), as opposed to a planned, clinician-led extubation. This is a clinical *finding* (recorded as an observation), not a procedure. Synonyms: "Accidental tracheal extubation".

## Excluded Concepts

In SNOMED, "changing a tube" is modelled as a *child* of "removing a tube", so it would otherwise be pulled in as a descendant of *Extubation of trachea*. A tube change replaces the tube immediately and leaves the patient with a continuous airway, so it does not represent an extubation. The following are therefore excluded (per issue #13):

- *4026054 / 225719006 — Changing endotracheal tube (SNOMED)* — and its descendant *4026055 / 225720000 — Changing endotracheal tube headband (SNOMED)*, which concerns the tube's external fixation, not the tube itself.
- *4331311 / 2267008 — Changing tracheostomy tube (SNOMED)*. Synonyms: "Replacement of tracheostomy tube", "Change of tracheostomy tube".

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records an extubation but does not say which device was removed or under what circumstances: *4148972 / 309812005 — Extubation of trachea*.
- **By device**: map to *4150627 / 271280005 — Removal of endotracheal tube* for an endotracheal tube, or *4012607 / 113153002 — Removal of tracheostomy tube* for tracheostomy decannulation, only when the source explicitly identifies the device. If the device is not documented, use the generic *4148972 / 309812005 — Extubation of trachea*.
- **Planned vs unplanned**: map to *4231838 / 405639008 — Inadvertent tracheal extubation* only when the source explicitly flags the event as accidental, unplanned, or self-extubation. A plain "extubation" with no such flag is a planned extubation and maps to *4148972 / 309812005 — Extubation of trachea*.
- **Do not map tube changes here**: source events labelled "tube change", "tube replacement", "ETT change", "trach change", or "change of tracheostomy tube" are *not* extubations and must not be mapped to this set, even though SNOMED files them under the removal hierarchy.
- **Reintubation / weaning context**: this set captures the extubation event itself. It does not capture the decision to wean, spontaneous breathing trials, or reintubation — those belong to separate concept sets.

fr.name: Extubation
fr.shortDescription: Retrait d’un dispositif des voies aériennes (sonde d’intubation ou de trachéotomie), y compris retrait accidentel ; les changements de sonde sont exclus.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Re-anchor on 'Extubation of trachea' with descendants and exclude the tube-change branch (issue #13): removed 'Changing endotracheal tube' (4026054), 'Changing endotracheal tube headband' (4026055) and 'Changing tracheostomy tube' (4331311); picked up 'Ventilating tube removal by another physician' (4195943). Added clinical description.
